CASE-2026-00042: Jordan Smith relocated from Birmingham to Manchester in December 2025. Evidence was requested on 15 January — proof of address received on 2 February, but the income statement remains outstanding after 91 days. The case has passed both the 28-day reminder threshold and the 56-day escalation threshold defined in POL-BR-003.
CASE-2026-00091: Aisha Okafor applied for an event licence for an annual community festival with a capacity of 500. All supporting documents were submitted with the application, identity was verified, and a premises inspection on 5 March found no concerns. The case is now awaiting a decision from a senior caseworker.
CASE-2026-00107: A compliance check on Meridian Care Solutions Ltd was triggered by a third-party referral in January 2026. A site visit on 10 March identified two serious regulatory breaches: staff operating without required qualifications and six months of record-keeping failures. The case was escalated to a senior officer the following day and enforcement options are under consideration.
CASE-2026-00133: Callum Rafferty's benefit review was triggered by a change from full-time to part-time employment (22 hours per week). All requested evidence — P45, new employer details, and updated income statement — was received and verified by 19 February. The new income falls below the threshold, and the draft decision recommends an award adjustment. This is a straightforward case awaiting team leader sign-off.
CASE-2026-00158: Priya Mehta's benefit review is part of the annual reassessment cycle. An evidence request for a medical certificate and current income statement was sent on 5 March 2026. No response has been received, but the 28-day reminder threshold has not yet been reached — the reminder will become due around 2 April.
CASE-2026-00172: The Old Crown Ltd applied for a premises licence renewal. The application had a clean five-year history, all documents were verified with no objections, and the renewal was approved without conditions. The licence was issued and the applicant was notified by letter on 28 February 2026. This case is closed.
CASE-2026-00199: A routine compliance check on Greenfield Training Academy was opened on 15 March 2026 as part of the annual inspection programme. Evidence — course delivery records, learner outcomes data, and tutor qualification certificates — was requested on 18 March. The organisation was last inspected 18 months ago with no issues. The 28-day response window is still in progress.
CASE-2026-00214: Marcus Okonkwo's benefit review was triggered by a change of address in November 2025. Evidence has been outstanding for over 150 days despite two reminder letters sent at days 28 and 42. The applicant's telephone number on record appears to be incorrect. The case was escalated to a team leader on 20 January 2026 after exceeding the 56-day threshold, and has remained in escalation since.
CASE-2026-00231: Riverside Events Ltd applied for a new premises licence for an outdoor venue. All required documents were submitted, and a 28-day public consultation was opened on 14 March. Two objections were received from local residents, both relating to potential noise impact. The applicant submitted an updated noise management plan in response. Per POL-LA-003, two or more substantive objections require senior caseworker review before a decision is issued.
CASE-2026-00248: A compliance check on Hartwood Construction plc was triggered by an anonymous whistleblower report alleging inadequate health and safety practices. Documentation was received on 19 March and appears largely compliant, but one gap has been noted — no incident log entries for a six-week period in autumn 2025. The organisation has been asked for clarification.
